research_question: Does the previously frozen market-residual momentum signal retain its net return and risk advantage when formation and rebalancing are reduced from monthly to calendar-quarterly frequency?
null_hypothesis: The quarterly strategy fails at least one frozen discovery gate relative to its same-date eligible-stock benchmark.

universe.scope: the same 46 current Top-50-derived qualified US stocks frozen by the input identity

signal.formation_session: last SPY trading session of each formation month
signal.beta_model: OLS of stock daily total return on intercept and SPY daily total return
signal.beta_estimation_window: 504 daily returns ending t-253 inclusive; minimum 450 paired observations
signal.momentum_window: 232 daily returns from t-252 through t-21 inclusive; minimum 220 paired observations
signal.score: mean residual divided by sample standard deviation of residuals, multiplied by sqrt(252)
signal.ranking: descending score with ascending symbol tie-break

portfolio.benchmark: equal weight all residual-score-eligible qualified stocks at the same formation
portfolio.entry: next SPY trading session close after formation
portfolio.holding_period: until the next calendar-quarterly execution close

costs.turnover_definition: 0.5 times the sum of absolute target-weight changes; initial entry excluded from annual turnover but charged costs

forbidden_changes: change selected count or retention buffer
try monthly, bimonthly, semiannual, or annual alternatives
add volatility, quality, market-state, or fundamental filters

conditional_pit_validation.required_upgrade: historical point-in-time Top-50 membership or an explicitly frozen reproducible PIT market-cap reconstruction
